rulings.Case Title: JODIE D. COLLINS SR. et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Marjorie Collins As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion to appoint Marjorie Collins as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff Jodie Collins, Sr. and for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KEAYS ET AL VS. 50 BEALE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 22, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's general demurrer is overruled as plaintiff's FAC pleads facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Specifically, plaintiffs complied with this Court's prior order granting them leave to amend to identify the product(s) for which defendants are allegedly responsible. The FAC is not so uncertain that defendant cannot reasonably respond. Defendant's special demurrers to the First (negligence) and Fifth (loss of consortium) causes of action are overruled. The FAC states facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for negligence and loss of consortium and are not uncertain. Defendant's special demurrer to the Third (strict liability) cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute that cause of action. Plaintiffs have 10 days leave to amend to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for strict liability, if they are able to do so, so as to state facts to show that defendant manufactured, supplied, distributed, placed into the stream of commerce or was in any way in the chain of distribution of its asbestos-containing products or could be liable under a strict liability theory in any other manner. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
